failure_surface: Code execution failed with SyntaxError due to malformed code block formatting; the script never ran successfully to process spreadsheet data
root_cause: The agent generated tool calls with improperly formatted code blocks that contained syntax errors at the entry point (incorrect prefix characters like 'code>' appearing where Python syntax should begin), preventing execution validation
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure all generated code blocks use valid Python syntax from the first line without extraneous markers or formatting artifacts before submission to execution tools
evidence: Multiple failed attempts show identical SyntaxError at 'Cell In[1], line 1' with '^' pointing to invalid syntax marker
All code submissions began with 'code>' prefix which is not valid Python syntax
No successful execution occurred - evaluation score was 0 with hard_restriction = 0
The openpyxl logic itself appeared correct but was never tested due to execution failure

memory_items.title: Validate Code Block Formatting Before Submission
memory_items.lesson: Before submitting code for execution, verify the entire code block starts with valid Python syntax and contains no extraneous markers or formatting artifacts that would cause immediate parse errors
memory_items.generalization: Tool calls containing executable code must pass basic syntax validation independently of the underlying logic correctness

memory_items.title: Use Dynamic Row Detection Over Hardcoded Ranges
memory_items.lesson: When iterating over spreadsheet rows for conditional logic changes, derive the iteration range from the actual worksheet's max_row property or detect data boundaries dynamically rather than using fixed numeric ranges
memory_items.generalization: Hardcoded row ranges may fail if data extends beyond expected bounds or if header/footer assumptions prove incorrect across different files

memory_items.title: Apply Correct Fill Properties for Background Color Clearing
memory_items.lesson: To remove cell background coloring via openpyxl, set fill_type to an appropriate value (typically None or solid with white) along with matching start_color and end_color values consistently
memory_items.generalization: Excel styling properties must be set with compatible combinations; mismatched fill types and colors may produce unexpected visual results even after save

edits.content:
## Execution Hygiene

### Code Validation
- Verify all submitted code passes basic syntax validation before tool invocation.
- Ensure code blocks begin with valid language syntax without extraneous prefixes or markers.
- Test code snippets locally or in a sandbox environment when possible before final submission.

### Data Range Handling
- Derive iteration ranges from dynamic sheet properties (e.g., ws.max_row) rather than fixed numeric bounds.
- Confirm data boundaries match instructions; scan for trailing rows or varying dataset sizes.

### Formatting Operations
- Apply styling properties with compatible combinations (fill type matches color settings).
- Test formatting changes against original cells to ensure intended visual outcomes.
